memoryArtifacts.headline: Lady Margaret de Multon de Darce (20 July 1300 – 10 December 1361)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Margaret de Multon, daughter of Thomas de Multon. 

She was born at Mulgrave Castle on July 20, 1300 and baptized July 24, 1300, at Lythe.

She married, before February 4, 1315/6, Randolf de Darce, af …

zh.Mulgrave Castle, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾姆爾格拉福 卡素
zh.Gilsland, Northumberland, England: 英國諾圖波藍德吉爾曼德
zh.St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, C: 英國波藍德萊納爾可斯特聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Multon於1300年7月20日出生在英國越可爾姆爾格拉福 卡素時，她的父親Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland24歲，母親Margaret Mauley17歲。她於1315年之前與Ranulph Dacre在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1361年12月10日在英國波藍德享年61歲，埋葬在英國波藍德萊納爾可斯特聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Multon于1300年7月20日出生在英国越可爾姆爾格拉福 卡素时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland24岁，母亲Margaret Mauley17岁。她于1315年之前与Ranulph Dacre在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1361年12月10日在英国波藍德享年61岁，埋葬在英国波藍德萊納爾可斯特聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Multon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Moulton
meanings: English (mainly Cheshire and Lancashire): habitational name from one or more of the places called Moulton (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire, Suffolk, North Yorkshire), most of which are named with the Old English personal name Mūl(a), the Old Norse personal name Múli, or Old English mūl ‘mule’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Multon was born on 20 July 1300, in Mulgrave Castle, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland, was 24 and her mother, Margaret Mauley, was 17. She married Ranulph Dacre before 1315, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died on 10 December 1361, in Cumberland, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, Cumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Multon est née le 20 juillet 1300, à Mulgrave Castle, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Mauley, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Ranulph Dacre avant 1315, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée le 10 décembre 1361, à Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, Cumberland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Multon am 20. Juli 1300 in Mulgrave Castle, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland 24 und ihre Mutter Margaret Mauley 17. Sie hat vor 1315 in Yorkshire, England Ranulph Dacre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb am 10. Dezember 1361 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Cumberland, England und wurde in St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, Cumberland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Multon è nata il il 20 luglio 1300 nel Mulgrave Castle, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Margaret Mauley, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Ranulph Dacre prima del 1315, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 10 dicembre 1361, nel Cumberland, England, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolta a St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, Cumberland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Multonが1300年7月20日にイギリスヨークシュアイアムルグラブ カストルで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilslandは24歳，母Margaret Mauleyは17歳であった。1315年以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアでRanulph Dacreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1361年12月10日にイギリスクウンバーランドで61歳で死去し，イギリスクウンバーランドランエルコストスト マリー マグダリーネ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, C: 영국 쿠벌랜드 랜르코스트 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Multon(이)가 1300년 7월 20일 영국 요르크실르 뮐그레이브 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Mauley(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1315년 이전 영국 요르크실르에서 Ranulph Dacre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 12월 10일 영국 쿠벌랜드에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 쿠벌랜드 랜르코스트 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Multon nasceu em 20 de julho de 1300, em Mulgrave Castle, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Mauley, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Ranulph Dacre antes 1315, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 10 de dezembro de 1361, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, Cumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary Magdalene's Church, Lanercost, C: ст мэри магдеилине'с чурч, ланиркост, кумбирланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Multon родилась 20 июля 1300 г. в местности мулграв кастл, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Multon IV of Gilsland было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Mauley, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ranulph Dacre перед 1315 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла 10 декабря 1361 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности ст мэри магдеилине'с чурч, ланиркост, кумбирланд, Англия.
